---
# EDIT AT YOUR OWN PERIL #
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    comments:
      hypothesis: true
    citeproc: true
    csl: journal-of-the-acm.csl
  allegheny-cis-pdf: default

natbiboptions: numbers
# EDIT AT YOUR OWN PERIL #

title: "WorkoutEvaluator: An iOS App That Provides Personalized Exercise Feedback Through User-provided Data"


firstreader: Gregory Kapfhammer 
secondreader: Oliver Bonham-Carter

bibliography: references.bib

author:
  - name: Evan Nelson
    affiliation:
      name: Allegheny College
      address: 520 N. Main St., Box Q
      city: Meadville
      state: Pennsylvania
      country: USA
      postal-code: 16335

abstract: |
This project presents WorkoutEvaluator, a fully developed iOS application designed to improve strength training by providing
structured workout tracking, performance visualization, and personalized feedback based entirely on user-entered data. The 
motivation for this work stems from limitations in existing fitness applications, which often rely on wearable sensors or 
lack meaningful, individualized insights for resistance training; this application instead focuses on simplicity, accessibility, 
and actionable analytics. Built using Swift and SwiftUI in Xcode, the system implements a lightweight architecture with local 
data persistence via JSON encoding and UserDefaults, and includes core features such as exercise logging, progress charts, 
evaluation metrics, achievement-based gamification, and guided exercise selection. To evaluate performance and scalability, 
controlled experiments were conducted using synthetic datasets ranging from 100 to 10,000 workout entries, measuring load times, 
query efficiency, and chart rendering responsiveness across multiple runs. Results demonstrate that the application maintains 
fast load and query times and remains responsive even at larger data scales, supporting its practicality for real-world use. 
Overall, this work delivers a functional, efficient, and user-focused fitness application that not only tracks workouts but 
actively helps users understand and improve their performance, offering a compelling alternative to existing solutions in the 
health and fitness app space.
---

{{< include _introduction.qmd >}}

{{< include _related_work.qmd >}}

{{< include _methods.qmd >}}

{{< include _experiments.qmd >}}

{{< include _conclusion.qmd >}}
